entities.name: GROUNDNUT
entities.description: The legume crop being evaluated for disease resistance.

entities.name: ROBUT 33-1
entities.description: A specific groundnut cultivar used as the subject for screening stem rot resistance.

entities.name: STEM ROT
entities.description: A fungal disease affecting groundnut plants, caused by Sclerotium rolfsii.

entities.name: STEM ROT RESISTANCE
entities.description: The capacity of a groundnut plant to withstand or recover from stem rot infection.

entities.name: SCLEROTIUM ROLFSII
entities.description: The fungal pathogen that causes stem rot in groundnut.

entities.name: CORTICIUM ROLFSII
entities.description: An alternative scientific name for the pathogen Sclerotium rolfsii.

entities.name: INOCULATION METHODS
entities.description: Various techniques used to introduce the pathogen to the plant to test for resistance.

entities.name: GREENHOUSE
entities.description: The controlled environment used to conduct the screening experiments.

entities.name: TEMPERATURE
entities.description: The thermal condition in the greenhouse, ranging from 16-31°C.

entities.name: HUMIDITY
entities.description: The moisture level in the greenhouse air, ranging from 40-54.5%.

entities.name: MORTALITY
entities.description: The rate of plant death used to quantify the severity of stem rot.

entities.name: ORGANIC MATTER
entities.description: Material used to cover the inoculum on the soil surface to optimize infection.
